title: toxicodendron radicans var. divaricatum
intro: Toxicodendron radicans var. divaricatum, also known as Poison Ivy with Divaricating Branchlets, is a plant that belongs to the Anacardiaceae family. It is known for its toxic properties, containing urushiol, an oil that causes severe skin irritation, rash, and blisters in humans and animals. Despite its toxic properties, the plant has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, and is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever and pain. The plant is typically prepared as a tea or tincture for medicinal use, although it is essential to exercise extreme caution when handling the plant due to its toxic nature.
